metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/golden/cli_help/transition__dashhelp.out` and `tests/golden/cli_help/transition__help.out` to the sweep's explicit edit targets, and extend AC1's grep scope to include `tests/golden/`.
findings.finding: The golden test fixtures `tests/golden/cli_help/transition__dashhelp.out` and `tests/golden/cli_help/transition__help.out` both contain `require_signature_for_close` and are not named in the sweep list. The AC says 'CLI help golden fixtures regenerated and consistent' but does not explicitly list these files, and they fall outside `docs/` and `src/rebar/_cli/help/` — the only two help paths the plan enumerates. A repo-wide grep for `require_signature_for_close` across `*.md` (as AC1 specifies) would miss them, but the fixtures are the authoritative rendered output the help text is regenerated from.
findings.location: AC item 4 — 'CLI help (transition etc.) and golden help fixtures regenerated and consistent'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'including `tests/golden/cli_help/`' and add regeneration of these two files to the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` to both the What section's key list and the AC grep list, and name its removal story dependency (or confirm it is handled by a separate sweep task).
findings.finding: The plan's key list and AC grep list both omit `emit_legacy_signature_mirror`, which the parent epic (8d27) explicitly identifies as one of the seven approved removals needing a sweep. The key is still live in _config_schema.py:367, _commands/compact.py:438/443/583, and docs/migrations.md:163/169. No removal story for it is named in the plan, and it is absent from the AC's grep list — meaning a doc-accuracy sweep that claims to cover all removed keys would leave stale migration doc references unswept.
findings.location: What section (removed key list) and Acceptance Criteria bullet 1 (grep list)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC bullet 1: '...`novelty_drop_active`, `emit_legacy_signature_mirror`) returns ZERO stale references...' and add a What-section entry pointing at docs/migrations.md:163,169 and any CLI help references.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` to the What sweep list and to AC #1's grep enumeration, covering at minimum `docs/migrations.md:163,169` and any other hits from a full-repo grep.
findings.finding: The leaf omits `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` from its sweep scope, contradicting the parent epic's requirement to purge all seven removed keys from docs/help/release-notes.
findings.location: What section (per-key sweep list) and AC #1 (grep enumeration)
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a bullet: '- `emit_legacy_signature_mirror`: `docs/migrations.md:163,169`, `docs/config.md` (if listed), any release-note or CLI-help references.' In AC #1, extend the grep enumeration to include `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` as the seventh key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` to both the What sweep list and AC#1's grep enumeration, and identify its known doc locations (`docs/migrations.md:163,169`) as explicit targets.
findings.finding: The sweep-target key set (the enum the executor must grep for) is missing `emit_legacy_signature_mirror`, a seventh removed key with live doc references already confirmed in the repo. The What section enumerates only 6 keys and AC#1's grep list likewise contains only 6 — meaning the sweep closes without ever checking or purging `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` references in `docs/migrations.md` (lines 163, 169) and test files.
findings.location: What section (per-key sweep list); AC#1 grep enumeration
findings.suggested_fix: In AC#1, extend the grep list to: `require_signature_for_close, baseline_dual_write, baseline_consumer_swap, progressive_drift_refresh, remediation_mode, novelty_drop_active, emit_legacy_signature_mirror`. In What, add a bullet: '- `emit_legacy_signature_mirror`: `docs/migrations.md:163,169`.' Note that this key may need its own retirement story before the sweep can zero it, or the sweep story must coordinate with that retirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the What section an explicit step describing HOW to regenerate CLI golden help fixtures (e.g. the command/make target), so the regeneration work in AC4 is fully described and executable.
findings.finding: AC4 specifies that 'CLI help golden help fixtures [are] regenerated and consistent,' but the plan body describes only a grep sweep and line-level edits to help files — it never describes the process, command, or script used to regenerate golden fixtures, leaving the regeneration step as uncovered work with no described deliverable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; What section (CLI help references)
findings.suggested_fix: Under the What section, add a bullet such as: 'Run `make regen-help-fixtures` (or equivalent) after all edits so golden fixtures are updated; commit the result and confirm CI passes the fixture-consistency check.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` sweep to cover all occurrences of `baseline_dual_write` and `baseline_consumer_swap`, including the troubleshooting section at lines 472–483 (and the section header at line 317).
findings.finding: The plan cites `docs/jira-sync-setup.md:329-348` as the only location to sweep for `baseline_dual_write` / `baseline_consumer_swap`, but actual repo occurrences extend to lines 317, 323, 335, 344, 347, 472, and 483. Lines 472 and 483 are outside the named range and will be missed.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '`docs/jira-sync-setup.md:329-348` (the phased-rollout section)' with '`docs/jira-sync-setup.md` (all occurrences: lines 317, 323, 329–348, 472–483)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the regeneration command and prove-consistency step for AC4 (e.g., `make regen-help && git diff --exit-code src/rebar/_cli/help/`) so the criterion is mechanically falsifiable.
findings.finding: AC4 ('CLI help regenerated and consistent') uses the subjective term 'consistent' without naming what the help is consistent WITH, and cites no regeneration command, leaving the criterion unverifiable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC4 with: 'Run `<regeneration-command>` then `git diff --exit-code src/rebar/_cli/help/` returns clean (no diff); CI golden-fixture test passes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the explicit ticket ID for the `require_signature_for_close` retirement story to the Scope/Depends line so the sequencing dependency is trackable (epic 8d27 lists it as one of four removal stories but the plan names only d6bd and 4cdf).
findings.finding: The plan's Scope states it 'Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, and the require_signature retirement having landed' — but no ticket ID is given for the require_signature_for_close retirement story. The key is still fully live in _config_schema.py:210, gates.py:60/79, txn.py:322, and all cited doc files; story d6bd covers only baseline_dual_write/baseline_consumer_swap and story 4cdf covers only the three verify-defaults — neither removes require_signature_for_close. Without a named, trackable dependency ticket this sweep story could execute (or be ordered) against a codebase where require_signature_for_close is still wired, making the doc cleanup either premature or incomplete.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, and the require_signature retirement having landed'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, and the require_signature retirement having landed' to 'Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, <ID-of-require_signature-retirement-story>, and the emit_legacy_signature_mirror removal (or the epic 8d27 closing)' and link all four predecessor tickets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `require_signature_for_close` retirement as an explicit named, blocking dependency (with a ticket ID) in the Scope/Depends-on section, and confirm it is a hard prereq before this story can begin.
findings.finding: The plan's ordering mechanism has an untracked dependency: it relies on 'the require_signature_for_close retirement having landed' but names only two upstream stories (d6bd and 4cdf) as explicit deps. Neither d6bd nor 4cdf removes `require_signature_for_close` — that key is still fully live in `_config_schema.py`, `gates.py`, `txn.py`, and `transition.txt`. Without a named, blocking dep for that third retirement story, the implementer could run the sweep before it lands and delete accurate documentation for a feature that is still active.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, and the require_signature retirement having landed'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, change 'Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, and the require_signature retirement having landed' to 'Depends on stories d6bd, 4cdf, and <ticket-id> (require_signature_for_close retirement) having landed' and add <ticket-id> to the formal deps list so the story is blocked until all three land.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that names the specific doc location(s) (e.g. `docs/config.md`, `docs/manifest-signing.md`) where each KEEP decision (signature mirror, completion floor) must remain documented, and specify the minimum content (param name, type, default, effect).
findings.finding: The 'What' section instructs authors to 'record the KEEP decisions (signature mirror, completion floor) where docs discuss them,' but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that these retained-param docs are accurate and present after the sweep. AC2 only checks that removed-key toggles are gone and 'retained tuning params are still documented' — it does not specify where or what that documentation must say, leaving the KEEP-decision documentation unverifiable.
findings.location: What section ('record the KEEP decisions…'); AC line 2 ('retained tuning params are still documented')
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'Each retained tuning parameter (signature mirror threshold, completion floor) has an entry in `docs/config.md` with name, type, default, and behavioral description intact after the sweep.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify for `docs/migrations.md:142` whether the reference should be deleted (stale/incorrect) or updated to a 'key removed in vX.Y, no replacement' notice, and add a note in the AC or What section protecting legitimate migration-history entries from the grep-to-zero rule.
findings.finding: The plan lists `docs/migrations.md:142` as a location to purge a stale `require_signature_for_close` reference, but does not distinguish between (a) an incorrect inline usage that should be deleted and (b) a migration-history entry telling operators 'this key was removed in vX.Y' that should be updated, not deleted. Deleting a migration-guide entry could leave operators without upgrade guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note: 'For `docs/migrations.md`, stale config-usage examples should be removed, but any entry recording the key's removal date/version should be updated (not deleted) to accurately state the key is retired.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the What section whether ADR historical prose describing removed back-out paths should be (a) updated inline using the existing update-note convention (matching the `(Update 2026-07-11: ...)` pattern already in ADR 0002/0008) or (b) stripped — and apply that choice consistently across all affected ADR files.
findings.finding: The grep-for-zero mechanism will surface hits in ADR narrative text (`docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:64,93` and `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md:90`) for the keys being hardcoded, but the plan gives no guidance on how to handle historical ADR prose — deleting 'explicit false backs out' or adding an amendment note are structurally different choices that affect the integrity of the ADR record. The What section's generic 'correct any doc statements that become inaccurate' is the only instruction, which is too vague for ADR history.
findings.location: What section: 'correct any doc statements that become inaccurate once behavior is hardcoded'; docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:64,93; docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md:90
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'For ADR files, add an inline update note (following the existing `(Update YYYY-MM-DD: ...)` convention) noting that the flag has been hardcoded always-on and removed; do not delete the historical back-out rationale text.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a decision rule to the What section specifying how ADR files containing toggle-framing for the removed keys should be handled (e.g., append a dated '> Update <date>: flag removed; behavior hardcoded always-on since story <id>' note rather than deleting historical rationale).
findings.finding: The plan's correction instruction ('"default on; explicit false is the back-out" must no longer imply a toggle') has no decision rule for ADR files, which contain exactly that framing for all three `verify.*` flags being hardcoded. AC#1's grep would flag these as stale hits requiring deletion, but the plan gives no guidance on whether ADR text should be deleted or replaced with 'hardcoded always-on since <story>' prose — leaving the executor without a protocol for the ADR case.
findings.location: What section ('correct any doc statements that become inaccurate once behavior is hardcoded'); AC#1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify whether `require_signature_for_close` has a dedicated retirement story (not d6bd or 4cdf); if yes, add it to `Depends on`; if no, either add a third sibling story or explicitly document that the key is KEPT and remove it from the sweep list.
findings.finding: The plan's scope statement ('docs/help/release-notes only') and AC grep scope ('docs/, *.md, CLI help') together leave unenumerated source-code and test consumers of `require_signature_for_close` that contain this key: `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` (lines 210, 485), `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py` (lines 60, 79), `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` (line 322), `README.md` (line 549), `CLAUDE.md` (line 272), `rebar.toml` (line 10 comment), and 10+ test files. No dependent ticket (d6bd or 4cdf) covers retirement of `require_signature_for_close`; it is not one of the three verify flags in 4cdf and not a reconciler flag in d6bd. The AC criterion 'ZERO stale references' across `*.md` would still pass, but a broader grep (which AC does NOT limit to docs-only) would surface these, and the schema/code still defines the key post-sweep.
findings.location: Scope section ('Docs/help/release-notes only') and AC item 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md`, `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md`, `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md`, and `docs/review-kernel.md` to the sweep list for `progressive_drift_refresh`, `remediation_mode`, and `novelty_drop_active`.
findings.finding: The plan names `docs/config.md` and `docs/plan-review-gate.md` for `progressive_drift_refresh` / `remediation_mode` / `novelty_drop_active`, but omits three ADR files that also contain these keys: `docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md` (references all three), `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` (remediation_mode, novelty_drop_active), and `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` (novelty_drop_active). Additionally, `docs/review-kernel.md:124` references `novelty_drop_active` and is unlisted.
findings.location: What section — 'progressive_drift_refresh / remediation_mode / novelty_drop_active: docs/config.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md, docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md if referenced'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the specific retained tuning params (e.g., `signature_mirror`, `completion_floor`) in AC2 and add a proving command (e.g., `grep -c 'completion_floor' docs/config.md` returns ≥1) so the 'still documented' clause is falsifiable.
findings.finding: AC2's second clause ('the retained tuning params are still documented') names no specific params, no target docs, and provides no proving command — an agent cannot verify this clause without guessing which params count.
findings.suggested_fix: Expand AC2 to: 'The retained tuning params — at minimum `<param_a>` and `<param_b>` — each appear at least once in `docs/config.md`; verify with `grep -c "<param_name>" docs/config.md` returning ≥1 for each.'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC3, e.g., extract the key list from the pruned schema and diff against `docs/config.md`, or provide a script/step that produces a diff with zero lines.
findings.finding: AC3 ('docs/config.md matches the pruned schema exactly') has no described proving command or artifact; 'matches exactly' is an unverifiable claim without a mechanism to compare the doc's key list to the pruned schema.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step such as: 'Run `python scripts/check_config_doc.py --schema src/rebar/config/schema.py --doc docs/config.md` and assert exit code 0; or diff `grep -E "^### " docs/config.md` against `python -c "import schema; print(schema.all_keys())"`.'`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
